Back to top
View this page

Workers¶

Workers are execution units that receive validation jobs and run them using the ValidationEngine. They’re deployed to compute backends (Lambda, K8s, Cloud Run).

Note

Workers receive jobs from the Orchestration module dispatchers. The orchestrator splits jobs by runtime type and dispatches to workers.

Overview¶

┌──────────────────┐
│    Orchestrator  │
│   (via registry) │
└────────┬─────────┘
         │ dispatch
         ▼
┌──────────────────┐     callback     ┌──────────────┐
│     Worker       │ ───────────────► │   Registry   │
│  (Lambda/K8s/    │                  │ (store       │
│   Cloud Run)     │                  │  results)    │
└──────────────────┘                  └──────────────┘

WasmWorker¶

Dedicated WASM-only worker for running inside containers. Used by the orchestrator to avoid Docker-in-Docker.

from griot_core.workers import WasmWorker

# Container entrypoint
async def main():
    worker = WasmWorker()
    result = await worker.run()  # Reads spec from env vars

# Or with explicit spec
worker = WasmWorker()
result = await worker.execute_wasm_checks(wasm_job_spec)

Environment Variables:

  • GRIOT_JOB_SPEC: JSON WasmJobSpec

  • GRIOT_JOB_ID: Job identifier

  • GRIOT_CALLBACK_URL: URL to POST results

LocalWorker¶

For development and testing.

from griot_core.workers import LocalWorker, run_local_validation

# Quick validation
result = await run_local_validation(
    contract=contract,
    data=arrow_data,
    profile="data_engineering",
)

# Or with worker
worker = LocalWorker(config)
result = await worker.execute(payload)

LambdaWorker¶

AWS Lambda handler.

from griot_core.workers import lambda_handler

# Lambda handler
def handler(event, context):
    return lambda_handler(event, context)

Deployment:

# serverless.yml
functions:
  griot-wasm-worker:
    handler: handler.handler
    timeout: 600
    memorySize: 1024
    environment:
      GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL: https://registry.example.com

KubernetesWorker¶

K8s Job execution.

from griot_core.workers import KubernetesWorker

# Container entrypoint
worker = KubernetesWorker(config)
await worker.run()

Environment Variables:

  • GRIOT_JOB_SPEC: Job specification

  • GRIOT_CALLBACK_URL: Callback URL

  • GRIOT_ARROW_DATA_PATH: Path to mounted Arrow data

CloudRunWorker¶

Google Cloud Run HTTP handler.

from griot_core.workers import CloudRunWorker

worker = CloudRunWorker(config)
app = worker.create_flask_app()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(host="0.0.0.0", port=8080)

Worker Types Comparison¶

Feature

Local

Lambda

K8s/Cloud Run

Use case

Development

Serverless

Production

Scaling

Single process

Auto-scale

Pod autoscaling

Cold start

None

Can be slow

Fast with pool

Max runtime

Unlimited

15 min

Configurable

Container checks

Limited

Lambda layers

Full support

API Reference¶

Griot Core Workers.

This module provides worker entrypoints for different compute environments. Workers are the execution units that receive validation jobs from the registry and run them using the ValidationEngine.

Supported compute environments: - Local: For development and testing - AWS Lambda: Serverless execution on AWS - Kubernetes: Container-based execution on K8s - Cloud Run: Serverless containers on GCP

Special workers: - WasmWorker: Dedicated WASM-only worker for running inside containers

(used by the orchestration module to avoid Docker-in-Docker)

class griot_core.workers.Worker(config)[source]¶

Abstract base class for workers.

Workers execute validation jobs received from the registry. They use the ValidationEngine to run checks and report results.

Parameters:

config (WorkerConfig)

__init__(config)[source]¶

Initialize the worker.

Parameters:

config (WorkerConfig) – Worker configuration

abstractmethod async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload with contract and data

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

abstractmethod async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

abstractmethod async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True if report was successful

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.workers.WorkerConfig(worker_id, worker_type='local', max_concurrent_jobs=1, default_timeout=300, registry_url=None, callback_enabled=True, extra_options=<factory>)[source]¶

Configuration for a worker.

Parameters:

  • worker_id (str)

  • worker_type (str)

  • max_concurrent_jobs (int)

  • default_timeout (int)

  • registry_url (str | None)

  • callback_enabled (bool)

  • extra_options (Dict[str, Any])

worker_id¶

Unique worker identifier

Type:

str

worker_type¶

Type of worker

Type:

str

max_concurrent_jobs¶

Maximum concurrent jobs

Type:

int

default_timeout¶

Default timeout in seconds

Type:

int

registry_url¶

URL of the Griot registry

Type:

str | None

callback_enabled¶

Whether to report results via callback

Type:

bool

extra_options¶

Additional worker-specific options

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

callback_enabled: bool = True¶
default_timeout: int = 300¶
max_concurrent_jobs: int = 1¶
registry_url: str | None = None¶
worker_type: str = 'local'¶
worker_id: str¶
extra_options: Dict[str, Any]¶
class griot_core.workers.WorkerResult(job_id, status, is_valid=False, started_at=None, completed_at=None, duration_ms=None, validation_result=None, errors=<factory>, worker_id=None, worker_type=None, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Result from a worker execution.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str)

  • status (WorkerStatus)

  • is_valid (bool)

  • started_at (datetime | None)

  • completed_at (datetime | None)

  • duration_ms (float | None)

  • validation_result (Dict[str, Any] | None)

  • errors (List[str])

  • worker_id (str | None)

  • worker_type (str | None)

  • metadata (Dict[str, Any])

job_id¶

Job identifier

Type:

str

status¶

Execution status

Type:

griot_core.workers.base.WorkerStatus

is_valid¶

Whether validation passed

Type:

bool

started_at¶

When execution started

Type:

datetime.datetime | None

completed_at¶

When execution completed

Type:

datetime.datetime | None

duration_ms¶

Execution duration in milliseconds

Type:

float | None

validation_result¶

Full validation result as dict

Type:

Dict[str, Any] | None

errors¶

List of error messages if failed

Type:

List[str]

worker_id¶

Identifier of the worker that executed

Type:

str | None

worker_type¶

Type of worker (local, lambda, k8s, cloudrun)

Type:

str | None

metadata¶

Additional result metadata

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

completed_at: datetime | None = None¶
duration_ms: float | None = None¶

Create from dictionary.

Parameters:

data (Dict[str, Any])

Return type:

WorkerResult

is_valid: bool = False¶
started_at: datetime | None = None¶

Convert to dictionary for serialization.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

Convert to JSON string.

Return type:

str

validation_result: Dict[str, Any] | None = None¶
worker_id: str | None = None¶
worker_type: str | None = None¶
job_id: str¶
status: WorkerStatus¶
errors: List[str]¶
metadata: Dict[str, Any]¶
class griot_core.workers.WorkerStatus(*values)[source]¶

Status of a worker execution.

PENDING = 'pending'¶
RUNNING = 'running'¶
COMPLETED = 'completed'¶
FAILED = 'failed'¶
TIMEOUT = 'timeout'¶
CANCELLED = 'cancelled'¶
class griot_core.workers.JobPayload(job_id, contract_id, contract_version=None, profile='default', environment='production', arrow_data=None, options=<factory>, callback_url=None, timeout_seconds=300, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Payload for a validation job.

This is the data structure passed to workers from the registry.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str)

  • contract_id (str)

  • contract_version (str | None)

  • profile (str)

  • environment (str)

  • arrow_data (Dict[str, bytes] | None)

  • options (Dict[str, Any])

  • callback_url (str | None)

  • timeout_seconds (int)

  • metadata (Dict[str, Any])

job_id¶

Unique job identifier

Type:

str

contract_id¶

Contract to validate

Type:

str

contract_version¶

Version of the contract

Type:

str | None

profile¶

Execution profile

Type:

str

environment¶

Environment (production, staging, dev)

Type:

str

arrow_data¶

Pre-fetched Arrow IPC data keyed by schema ID

Type:

Dict[str, bytes] | None

options¶

Additional validation options

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

callback_url¶

URL to report results back to registry

Type:

str | None

timeout_seconds¶

Maximum execution time

Type:

int

metadata¶

Additional job metadata

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

arrow_data: Dict[str, bytes] | None = None¶
callback_url: str | None = None¶
contract_version: str | None = None¶
environment: str = 'production'¶

data (Dict[str, Any])

JobPayload

class griot_core.workers.ContractFetcher(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Protocol for fetching contracts.

fetch(contract_id, version=None)[source]¶

Fetch a contract by ID and optional version.

Parameters:

  • contract_id (str)

  • version (str | None)

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

class griot_core.workers.LocalWorker(config=None, validation_engine=None, contract_fetcher=None)[source]¶

Local worker for development and testing.

Executes validation jobs synchronously in the local process. Useful for: - Development and debugging - Unit testing - CI/CD pipelines - Simple single-machine deployments

Example

>>> config = WorkerConfig(worker_id="local-1", worker_type="local")
>>> worker = LocalWorker(config)
>>> worker.contract_fetcher.add_contract(my_contract)
>>> result = await worker.execute(JobPayload(
...     job_id="job-1",
...     contract_id="my-contract",
...     profile="data_engineering",
... ))
>>> print(f"Valid: {result.is_valid}")
Parameters:

  • config (Optional[WorkerConfig])

  • validation_engine (Optional[ValidationEngine])

  • contract_fetcher (Optional[LocalContractFetcher])

__init__(config=None, validation_engine=None, contract_fetcher=None)[source]¶

Initialize the local worker.

Parameters:

  • config (WorkerConfig | None) – Worker configuration

  • validation_engine (ValidationEngine | None) – Optional pre-configured validation engine

  • contract_fetcher (LocalContractFetcher | None) – Optional contract fetcher

clear_results()[source]¶

Clear all stored results.

Return type:

None

async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job locally.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload with contract and data

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

get_result(job_id)[source]¶

Get a stored result by job ID.

Parameters:

job_id (str) – Job identifier

Returns:

WorkerResult if found, None otherwise

Return type:

WorkerResult | None

async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

list_results()[source]¶

List all stored results.

Returns:

Dictionary of job_id to WorkerResult

Return type:

Dict[str, WorkerResult]

async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry.

For local worker, this is a no-op by default. Override or configure for custom reporting.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True (always succeeds for local)

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.workers.LocalContractFetcher[source]¶

Simple contract fetcher for local testing.

Stores contracts in memory and retrieves them by ID.

add_contract(contract)[source]¶

Add a contract to the fetcher.

Parameters:

contract (Contract)

Return type:

None

add_contract_json(json_str)[source]¶

Parse and add a contract from JSON.

Parameters:

json_str (str)

Return type:

Contract

add_contract_yaml(yaml_str)[source]¶

Parse and add a contract from YAML.

Parameters:

yaml_str (str)

Return type:

Contract

fetch(contract_id, version=None)[source]¶

Fetch a contract by ID.

Parameters:

  • contract_id (str)

  • version (str | None)

Return type:

Contract

async griot_core.workers.run_local_validation(contract, profile='default', arrow_data=None, options=None)[source]¶

Convenience function to run validation locally.

Parameters:

  • contract (Contract) – Contract to validate

  • profile (str) – Execution profile

  • arrow_data (Dict[str, bytes] | None) – Arrow IPC data keyed by schema ID

  • options (Dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional validation options

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

Example

>>> result = await run_local_validation(
...     contract=my_contract,
...     profile="data_engineering",
...     arrow_data={"schema-1": arrow_bytes},
... )
>>> print(f"Valid: {result.is_valid}")
class griot_core.workers.LambdaWorker(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

AWS Lambda worker for serverless validation.

Designed to be invoked by AWS Lambda runtime. Handles: - Event parsing from various invocation sources - Contract fetching from registry or S3 - Validation execution - Result reporting via callback or response

Environment Variables:

GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL: URL of the Griot registry GRIOT_WORKER_ID: Worker identifier (defaults to Lambda function name) GRIOT_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT: Default timeout in seconds AWS_LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME: Lambda function name (set by Lambda)

Example Lambda handler:
>>> def handler(event, context):
...     worker = LambdaWorker()
...     return asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(
...         worker.handle_event(event, context)
...     )
Parameters:

  • config (Optional[WorkerConfig])

  • validation_engine (Optional[ValidationEngine])

__init__(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Initialize the Lambda worker.

Parameters:

  • config (WorkerConfig | None) – Worker configuration (defaults to env-based config)

  • validation_engine (ValidationEngine | None) – Optional pre-configured validation engine

async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

async handle_event(event, context=None)[source]¶

Handle a Lambda invocation event.

Supports multiple event sources: - Direct invocation with JobPayload - API Gateway (HTTP POST with JSON body) - SQS messages - EventBridge events

Parameters:

  • event (Dict[str, Any]) – Lambda event

  • context (Any) – Lambda context

Returns:

Response dictionary with results

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry via callback URL.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True if successful

Return type:

bool

griot_core.workers.lambda_handler(event, context)¶

AWS Lambda handler function.

This is the entry point for Lambda invocations.

Parameters:

  • event (Dict[str, Any]) – Lambda event

  • context (Any) – Lambda context

Returns:

Response dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

class griot_core.workers.KubernetesWorker(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Kubernetes worker for container-based validation.

Designed to run as a Kubernetes Job or standalone pod. Reads job configuration from environment variables or mounted ConfigMaps/Secrets.

Environment Variables:

GRIOT_JOB_ID: Job identifier GRIOT_CONTRACT_ID: Contract to validate GRIOT_CONTRACT_VERSION: Contract version (optional) GRIOT_PROFILE: Execution profile (default: “default”) GRIOT_ENVIRONMENT: Environment (default: “production”) GRIOT_CALLBACK_URL: URL to report results GRIOT_TIMEOUT: Timeout in seconds GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL: URL of the Griot registry GRIOT_WORKER_ID: Worker identifier GRIOT_ARROW_DATA_PATH: Path to mounted Arrow data directory KUBERNETES_POD_NAME: Pod name (set by K8s downward API) KUBERNETES_NAMESPACE: Namespace (set by K8s downward API)

Example Kubernetes Job:

apiVersion: batch/v1 kind: Job metadata:

name: griot-validation-job

spec:
template:
spec:

containers: - name: validator

image: griot/worker:latest env: - name: GRIOT_JOB_ID

value: “job-123”

  • name: GRIOT_CONTRACT_ID value: “my-contract”

volumeMounts: - name: arrow-data

mountPath: /data

restartPolicy: Never

backoffLimit: 2

Parameters:

  • config (Optional[WorkerConfig])

  • validation_engine (Optional[ValidationEngine])

__init__(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Initialize the Kubernetes worker.

Parameters:

  • config (WorkerConfig | None) – Worker configuration (defaults to env-based config)

  • validation_engine (ValidationEngine | None) – Optional pre-configured validation engine

async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

get_job_payload_from_env()[source]¶

Build JobPayload from environment variables.

Returns:

JobPayload configured from environment

Raises:

ValueError – If required environment variables are missing

Return type:

JobPayload

async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True if successful

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.workers.CloudRunWorker(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Google Cloud Run worker for serverless container validation.

Designed to run as a Cloud Run service that receives HTTP requests. Supports both synchronous and asynchronous (Pub/Sub) invocation.

Environment Variables:

GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL: URL of the Griot registry GRIOT_WORKER_ID: Worker identifier (defaults to K_REVISION) GRIOT_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT: Default timeout in seconds K_REVISION: Cloud Run revision name (set by Cloud Run) K_SERVICE: Cloud Run service name (set by Cloud Run) PORT: HTTP port (set by Cloud Run, default 8080)

Example Cloud Run Service:
gcloud run deploy griot-worker

–image gcr.io/project/griot-worker:latest –set-env-vars GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL=https://registry.example.com –memory 1Gi –timeout 300s

Parameters:

  • config (Optional[WorkerConfig])

  • validation_engine (Optional[ValidationEngine])

__init__(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Initialize the Cloud Run worker.

Parameters:

  • config (WorkerConfig | None) – Worker configuration (defaults to env-based config)

  • validation_engine (ValidationEngine | None) – Optional pre-configured validation engine

async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

async handle_request(request_data, headers=None)[source]¶

Handle an HTTP request.

Supports: - Direct HTTP POST with JSON body - Cloud Pub/Sub push messages - Cloud Tasks HTTP requests

Parameters:

  • request_data (Dict[str, Any]) – Request body as dictionary

  • headers (Dict[str, str] | None) – Optional request headers

Returns:

Response dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True if successful

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.workers.WasmWorker(wasm_cache_dir=None, callback_timeout=30.0)[source]¶

Dedicated WASM worker for executing WASM checks.

This worker is designed to run inside a container orchestrated by the ValidationOrchestrator. It:

  1. Receives a WasmJobSpec via environment variable

  2. Fetches data from the data_reference

  3. Executes all WASM checks sequentially

  4. POSTs results to the callback URL

Example usage in container entrypoint:

worker = WasmWorker() await worker.run()

Or execute specific checks:

spec = WasmJobSpec(…) result = await worker.execute_wasm_checks(spec)

Parameters:

  • wasm_cache_dir (Path | None)

  • callback_timeout (float)

__init__(wasm_cache_dir=None, callback_timeout=30.0)[source]¶

Initialize the WASM worker.

Parameters:

  • wasm_cache_dir (Path | None) – Directory for caching WASM modules

  • callback_timeout (float) – Timeout for callback POST requests

async execute_wasm_checks(spec)[source]¶

Execute WASM checks from a WasmJobSpec.

Parameters:

spec (Any) – WasmJobSpec instance

Returns:

Result dictionary

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

async execute_wasm_checks_from_dict(spec_dict)[source]¶

Execute WASM checks from a spec dictionary.

Parameters:

spec_dict (dict[str, Any]) – WasmJobSpec as dictionary

Returns:

WasmWorkerResult

Return type:

WasmWorkerResult

async run()[source]¶

Run the worker using spec from environment.

Reads GRIOT_JOB_SPEC from environment and executes checks. POSTs results to callback URL if provided.

Returns:

WasmWorkerResult

Return type:

WasmWorkerResult

class griot_core.workers.WasmCheckResult(check_name, passed, metric_value=None, threshold=None, operator=None, severity='warning', execution_time_ms=None, error=None, details=<factory>)[source]¶

Result of a single WASM check execution.

Parameters:

  • check_name (str)

  • passed (bool)

  • metric_value (float | None)

  • threshold (float | None)

  • operator (str | None)

  • severity (str)

  • execution_time_ms (float | None)

  • error (str | None)

  • details (dict[str, Any])

error: str | None = None¶
execution_time_ms: float | None = None¶
metric_value: float | None = None¶
operator: str | None = None¶
severity: str = 'warning'¶
threshold: float | None = None¶
to_dict()[source]¶

Convert to dictionary.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

check_name: str¶
passed: bool¶
details: dict[str, Any]¶

Result from WASM worker execution.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str)

  • success (bool)

  • check_results (list[WasmCheckResult])

  • started_at (datetime | None)

  • completed_at (datetime | None)

  • duration_ms (float | None)

  • error (str | None)

  • metadata (dict[str, Any])

duration_ms: float | None = None¶
error: str | None = None¶
started_at: datetime | None = None¶

Convert to dictionary for callback.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

job_id: str¶
success: bool¶
check_results: list[WasmCheckResult]¶
metadata: dict[str, Any]¶

Base worker types and protocols.

Defines the common interface for all worker implementations.

class griot_core.workers.base.WorkerStatus(*values)[source]¶

Status of a worker execution.

class griot_core.workers.base.JobPayload(job_id, contract_id, contract_version=None, profile='default', environment='production', arrow_data=None, options=<factory>, callback_url=None, timeout_seconds=300, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Payload for a validation job.

This is the data structure passed to workers from the registry.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str)

  • contract_id (str)

  • contract_version (str | None)

  • profile (str)

  • environment (str)

  • arrow_data (Dict[str, bytes] | None)

  • options (Dict[str, Any])

  • callback_url (str | None)

  • timeout_seconds (int)

  • metadata (Dict[str, Any])

Unique job identifier

str

contract_id¶

Contract to validate

Type:

str

contract_version¶

Version of the contract

Type:

str | None

profile¶

Execution profile

Type:

str

environment¶

Environment (production, staging, dev)

Type:

str

arrow_data¶

Pre-fetched Arrow IPC data keyed by schema ID

Type:

Dict[str, bytes] | None

options¶

Additional validation options

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

callback_url¶

URL to report results back to registry

Type:

str | None

timeout_seconds¶

Maximum execution time

Type:

int

metadata¶

Additional job metadata

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

class griot_core.workers.base.WorkerResult(job_id, status, is_valid=False, started_at=None, completed_at=None, duration_ms=None, validation_result=None, errors=<factory>, worker_id=None, worker_type=None, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Result from a worker execution.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str)

  • status (WorkerStatus)

  • is_valid (bool)

  • started_at (datetime | None)

  • completed_at (datetime | None)

  • duration_ms (float | None)

  • validation_result (Dict[str, Any] | None)

  • errors (List[str])

  • worker_id (str | None)

  • worker_type (str | None)

  • metadata (Dict[str, Any])

job_id¶

Job identifier

Type:

str

status¶

Execution status

Type:

griot_core.workers.base.WorkerStatus

is_valid¶

Whether validation passed

Type:

bool

started_at¶

When execution started

Type:

datetime.datetime | None

completed_at¶

When execution completed

Type:

datetime.datetime | None

duration_ms¶

Execution duration in milliseconds

Type:

float | None

validation_result¶

Full validation result as dict

Type:

Dict[str, Any] | None

errors¶

List of error messages if failed

Type:

List[str]

worker_id¶

Identifier of the worker that executed

Type:

str | None

worker_type¶

Type of worker (local, lambda, k8s, cloudrun)

Type:

str | None

metadata¶

Additional result metadata

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

to_dict()[source]¶

Convert to dictionary for serialization.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

to_json()[source]¶

Convert to JSON string.

Return type:

str

classmethod from_dict(data)[source]¶

Create from dictionary.

Parameters:

data (Dict[str, Any])

Return type:

WorkerResult

class griot_core.workers.base.WorkerConfig(worker_id, worker_type='local', max_concurrent_jobs=1, default_timeout=300, registry_url=None, callback_enabled=True, extra_options=<factory>)[source]¶

Configuration for a worker.

Parameters:

  • worker_id (str)

  • worker_type (str)

  • max_concurrent_jobs (int)

  • default_timeout (int)

  • registry_url (str | None)

  • callback_enabled (bool)

  • extra_options (Dict[str, Any])

worker_id¶

Unique worker identifier

Type:

str

worker_type¶

Type of worker

Type:

str

max_concurrent_jobs¶

Maximum concurrent jobs

Type:

int

default_timeout¶

Default timeout in seconds

Type:

int

registry_url¶

URL of the Griot registry

Type:

str | None

callback_enabled¶

Whether to report results via callback

Type:

bool

extra_options¶

Additional worker-specific options

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

class griot_core.workers.base.ContractFetcher(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Protocol for fetching contracts.

fetch(contract_id, version=None)[source]¶

Fetch a contract by ID and optional version.

Parameters:

  • contract_id (str)

  • version (str | None)

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

class griot_core.workers.base.Worker(config)[source]¶

Abstract base class for workers.

Workers execute validation jobs received from the registry. They use the ValidationEngine to run checks and report results.

Parameters:

config (WorkerConfig)

__init__(config)[source]¶

Initialize the worker.

Parameters:

config (WorkerConfig) – Worker configuration

abstractmethod async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload with contract and data

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

abstractmethod async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True if report was successful

Return type:

bool

abstractmethod async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

Dedicated WASM worker for running WASM checks inside containers.

This worker is designed to run inside a griot-core worker container (e.g., K8s Job). It receives a WasmJobSpec and executes all WASM checks sequentially using the embedded WASM runtime.

No Docker/Podman needed - WASM modules run in-process.

class griot_core.workers.wasm_worker.WasmCheckResult(check_name, passed, metric_value=None, threshold=None, operator=None, severity='warning', execution_time_ms=None, error=None, details=<factory>)[source]¶

Result of a single WASM check execution.

Parameters:

  • check_name (str)

  • passed (bool)

  • metric_value (float | None)

  • threshold (float | None)

  • operator (str | None)

  • severity (str)

  • execution_time_ms (float | None)

  • error (str | None)

  • details (dict[str, Any])

to_dict()[source]¶

Convert to dictionary.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

class griot_core.workers.wasm_worker.WasmWorkerResult(job_id, success, check_results, started_at=None, completed_at=None, duration_ms=None, error=None, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Result from WASM worker execution.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str)

  • success (bool)

  • check_results (list[WasmCheckResult])

  • started_at (datetime | None)

  • completed_at (datetime | None)

  • duration_ms (float | None)

  • error (str | None)

  • metadata (dict[str, Any])

to_dict()[source]¶

Convert to dictionary for callback.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

class griot_core.workers.wasm_worker.WasmWorker(wasm_cache_dir=None, callback_timeout=30.0)[source]¶

Dedicated WASM worker for executing WASM checks.

This worker is designed to run inside a container orchestrated by the ValidationOrchestrator. It:

  1. Receives a WasmJobSpec via environment variable

  2. Fetches data from the data_reference

  3. Executes all WASM checks sequentially

  4. POSTs results to the callback URL

Example usage in container entrypoint:

worker = WasmWorker() await worker.run()

Or execute specific checks:

spec = WasmJobSpec(…) result = await worker.execute_wasm_checks(spec)

Parameters:

  • wasm_cache_dir (Path | None)

  • callback_timeout (float)

__init__(wasm_cache_dir=None, callback_timeout=30.0)[source]¶

Initialize the WASM worker.

Parameters:

  • wasm_cache_dir (Path | None) – Directory for caching WASM modules

  • callback_timeout (float) – Timeout for callback POST requests

async run()[source]¶

Run the worker using spec from environment.

Reads GRIOT_JOB_SPEC from environment and executes checks. POSTs results to callback URL if provided.

Returns:

WasmWorkerResult

Return type:

WasmWorkerResult

async execute_wasm_checks(spec)[source]¶

Execute WASM checks from a WasmJobSpec.

Parameters:

spec (Any) – WasmJobSpec instance

Returns:

Result dictionary

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

async execute_wasm_checks_from_dict(spec_dict)[source]¶

Execute WASM checks from a spec dictionary.

Parameters:

spec_dict (dict[str, Any]) – WasmJobSpec as dictionary

Returns:

WasmWorkerResult

Return type:

WasmWorkerResult

async griot_core.workers.wasm_worker.main()[source]¶

Entry point for WASM worker container.

Local worker for development and testing.

Executes validation jobs in the local process, useful for development, testing, and debugging.

class griot_core.workers.local.LocalContractFetcher[source]¶

Simple contract fetcher for local testing.

Stores contracts in memory and retrieves them by ID.

add_contract(contract)[source]¶

Add a contract to the fetcher.

Parameters:

contract (Contract)

Return type:

None

add_contract_yaml(yaml_str)[source]¶

Parse and add a contract from YAML.

Parameters:

yaml_str (str)

Return type:

Contract

add_contract_json(json_str)[source]¶

Parse and add a contract from JSON.

Parameters:

json_str (str)

Return type:

Contract

fetch(contract_id, version=None)[source]¶

Fetch a contract by ID.

Parameters:

  • contract_id (str)

  • version (str | None)

Return type:

Contract

class griot_core.workers.local.LocalWorker(config=None, validation_engine=None, contract_fetcher=None)[source]¶

Local worker for development and testing.

Executes validation jobs synchronously in the local process. Useful for: - Development and debugging - Unit testing - CI/CD pipelines - Simple single-machine deployments

Example

>>> config = WorkerConfig(worker_id="local-1", worker_type="local")
>>> worker = LocalWorker(config)
>>> worker.contract_fetcher.add_contract(my_contract)
>>> result = await worker.execute(JobPayload(
...     job_id="job-1",
...     contract_id="my-contract",
...     profile="data_engineering",
... ))
>>> print(f"Valid: {result.is_valid}")
Parameters:

  • config (Optional[WorkerConfig])

  • validation_engine (Optional[ValidationEngine])

  • contract_fetcher (Optional[LocalContractFetcher])

__init__(config=None, validation_engine=None, contract_fetcher=None)[source]¶

Initialize the local worker.

Parameters:

  • config (WorkerConfig | None) – Worker configuration

  • validation_engine (ValidationEngine | None) – Optional pre-configured validation engine

  • contract_fetcher (LocalContractFetcher | None) – Optional contract fetcher

async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job locally.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload with contract and data

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry.

For local worker, this is a no-op by default. Override or configure for custom reporting.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True (always succeeds for local)

Return type:

bool

async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

get_result(job_id)[source]¶

Get a stored result by job ID.

Parameters:

job_id (str) – Job identifier

Returns:

WorkerResult if found, None otherwise

Return type:

WorkerResult | None

list_results()[source]¶

List all stored results.

Returns:

Dictionary of job_id to WorkerResult

Return type:

Dict[str, WorkerResult]

clear_results()[source]¶

Clear all stored results.

Return type:

None

async griot_core.workers.local.run_local_validation(contract, profile='default', arrow_data=None, options=None)[source]¶

Convenience function to run validation locally.

Parameters:

  • contract (Contract) – Contract to validate

  • profile (str) – Execution profile

  • arrow_data (Dict[str, bytes] | None) – Arrow IPC data keyed by schema ID

  • options (Dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional validation options

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

Example

>>> result = await run_local_validation(
...     contract=my_contract,
...     profile="data_engineering",
...     arrow_data={"schema-1": arrow_bytes},
... )
>>> print(f"Valid: {result.is_valid}")

AWS Lambda worker for serverless validation.

Provides the Lambda handler function and worker implementation for running validation jobs on AWS Lambda.

class griot_core.workers.lambda_worker.LambdaWorker(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

AWS Lambda worker for serverless validation.

Designed to be invoked by AWS Lambda runtime. Handles: - Event parsing from various invocation sources - Contract fetching from registry or S3 - Validation execution - Result reporting via callback or response

Environment Variables:

GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL: URL of the Griot registry GRIOT_WORKER_ID: Worker identifier (defaults to Lambda function name) GRIOT_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT: Default timeout in seconds AWS_LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME: Lambda function name (set by Lambda)

Example Lambda handler:
>>> def handler(event, context):
...     worker = LambdaWorker()
...     return asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(
...         worker.handle_event(event, context)
...     )
Parameters:

  • config (Optional[WorkerConfig])

  • validation_engine (Optional[ValidationEngine])

__init__(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Initialize the Lambda worker.

Parameters:

  • config (WorkerConfig | None) – Worker configuration (defaults to env-based config)

  • validation_engine (ValidationEngine | None) – Optional pre-configured validation engine

async handle_event(event, context=None)[source]¶

Handle a Lambda invocation event.

Supports multiple event sources: - Direct invocation with JobPayload - API Gateway (HTTP POST with JSON body) - SQS messages - EventBridge events

Parameters:

  • event (Dict[str, Any]) – Lambda event

  • context (Any) – Lambda context

Returns:

Response dictionary with results

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry via callback URL.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True if successful

Return type:

bool

async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

griot_core.workers.lambda_worker.handler(event, context)[source]¶

AWS Lambda handler function.

This is the entry point for Lambda invocations.

Parameters:

  • event (Dict[str, Any]) – Lambda event

  • context (Any) – Lambda context

Returns:

Response dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

Kubernetes worker for container-based validation.

Provides the worker implementation for running validation jobs as Kubernetes Jobs or standalone pods.

class griot_core.workers.kubernetes.KubernetesWorker(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Kubernetes worker for container-based validation.

Designed to run as a Kubernetes Job or standalone pod. Reads job configuration from environment variables or mounted ConfigMaps/Secrets.

Environment Variables:

GRIOT_JOB_ID: Job identifier GRIOT_CONTRACT_ID: Contract to validate GRIOT_CONTRACT_VERSION: Contract version (optional) GRIOT_PROFILE: Execution profile (default: “default”) GRIOT_ENVIRONMENT: Environment (default: “production”) GRIOT_CALLBACK_URL: URL to report results GRIOT_TIMEOUT: Timeout in seconds GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL: URL of the Griot registry GRIOT_WORKER_ID: Worker identifier GRIOT_ARROW_DATA_PATH: Path to mounted Arrow data directory KUBERNETES_POD_NAME: Pod name (set by K8s downward API) KUBERNETES_NAMESPACE: Namespace (set by K8s downward API)

Example Kubernetes Job:

apiVersion: batch/v1 kind: Job metadata:

name: griot-validation-job

spec:
template:
spec:

containers: - name: validator

image: griot/worker:latest env: - name: GRIOT_JOB_ID

value: “job-123”

  • name: GRIOT_CONTRACT_ID value: “my-contract”

volumeMounts: - name: arrow-data

mountPath: /data

restartPolicy: Never

backoffLimit: 2

Parameters:

  • config (Optional[WorkerConfig])

  • validation_engine (Optional[ValidationEngine])

__init__(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Initialize the Kubernetes worker.

Parameters:

  • config (WorkerConfig | None) – Worker configuration (defaults to env-based config)

  • validation_engine (ValidationEngine | None) – Optional pre-configured validation engine

get_job_payload_from_env()[source]¶

Build JobPayload from environment variables.

Returns:

JobPayload configured from environment

Raises:

ValueError – If required environment variables are missing

Return type:

JobPayload

async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True if successful

Return type:

bool

async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

griot_core.workers.kubernetes.main()[source]¶

Main entry point for Kubernetes worker.

Reads configuration from environment variables and runs validation.

Returns:

Exit code (0 for success, 1 for failure)

Return type:

int

Google Cloud Run worker for serverless container validation.

Provides the HTTP handler and worker implementation for running validation jobs on Google Cloud Run.

class griot_core.workers.cloudrun.CloudRunWorker(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Google Cloud Run worker for serverless container validation.

Designed to run as a Cloud Run service that receives HTTP requests. Supports both synchronous and asynchronous (Pub/Sub) invocation.

Environment Variables:

GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL: URL of the Griot registry GRIOT_WORKER_ID: Worker identifier (defaults to K_REVISION) GRIOT_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT: Default timeout in seconds K_REVISION: Cloud Run revision name (set by Cloud Run) K_SERVICE: Cloud Run service name (set by Cloud Run) PORT: HTTP port (set by Cloud Run, default 8080)

Example Cloud Run Service:
gcloud run deploy griot-worker

–image gcr.io/project/griot-worker:latest –set-env-vars GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL=https://registry.example.com –memory 1Gi –timeout 300s

Parameters:

  • config (Optional[WorkerConfig])

  • validation_engine (Optional[ValidationEngine])

__init__(config=None, validation_engine=None)[source]¶

Initialize the Cloud Run worker.

Parameters:

  • config (WorkerConfig | None) – Worker configuration (defaults to env-based config)

  • validation_engine (ValidationEngine | None) – Optional pre-configured validation engine

async handle_request(request_data, headers=None)[source]¶

Handle an HTTP request.

Supports: - Direct HTTP POST with JSON body - Cloud Pub/Sub push messages - Cloud Tasks HTTP requests

Parameters:

  • request_data (Dict[str, Any]) – Request body as dictionary

  • headers (Dict[str, str] | None) – Optional request headers

Returns:

Response dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

async execute(payload)[source]¶

Execute a validation job.

Parameters:

payload (JobPayload) – Job payload

Returns:

WorkerResult with validation results

Return type:

WorkerResult

async report_result(result)[source]¶

Report results back to the registry.

Parameters:

result (WorkerResult) – Worker result to report

Returns:

True if successful

Return type:

bool

async health_check()[source]¶

Check worker health.

Returns:

Health status dictionary

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

griot_core.workers.cloudrun.create_app()[source]¶

Create Flask application for Cloud Run.

Returns:

Flask application

griot_core.workers.cloudrun.main()[source]¶

Main entry point for Cloud Run.

Starts the Flask server on the configured port.
